Action: Inspect all site images using inspection tools or screen reader
Expected Result: Each image must have an alt attribute with meaningful description or alt='' if decorative

Action: Verify functional images (buttons, links) with accessibility inspector
Expected Result: Functional images must describe the action or destination, not just the visual image

Action: Review complex images (charts, diagrams) and look for extended descriptions
Expected Result: Complex images must have accessible long description via longdesc, aria-describedby, or adjacent text

Test Summary: TC - [A11y] - 1.2 - Time-based Media - 1.2.1 - Audio-only and Video-only (Prerecorded)
Action: Play audio-only or video-only content and verify available alternatives
Expected Result: A text transcript or accessible alternative description must exist alongside the content

Action: Verify that transcript includes speaker identification and relevant sounds
Expected Result: Transcript must be complete, identify who is speaking, and describe important sounds for comprehension

Action: Play prerecorded videos on the site
Expected Result: Videos must include synchronized activatable captions covering all dialogue and relevant sounds

Action: Verify caption synchronization and accuracy with audio
Expected Result: Captions must appear synchronized with audio, without significant errors, covering relevant sound effects

Test Summary: TC - [A11y] - 1.2 - Time-based Media - 1.2.3 - Audio Description or Media Alternative (Prerecorded)
Action: Play videos with essential visual content and verify descriptions
Expected Result: Audio description or text alternative must exist describing important visual information

Action: Verify that alternative covers all critical visual elements for comprehension
Expected Result: Description must include actions, expressions, scene changes, and unspoken on-screen text

Action: Access live broadcasts if available
Expected Result: Live broadcasts must have real-time captioning

Action: Verify accuracy and acceptable delay of live captions
Expected Result: Captions must reflect spoken audio with reasonable accuracy and minimal delay (maximum 3-5 seconds)

Action: Identify prerecorded videos containing essential visual information not conveyed in audio
Expected Result: Videos must include audio description covering actions, characters, scene changes, and relevant visual elements

Action: Play video with audio description enabled and verify synchronization
Expected Result: Audio description must be inserted in natural pauses without overlapping main audio

Action: Verify that audio description covers all critical visual elements
Expected Result: Essential visual information must be fully described for blind or low vision users

Action: Identify prerecorded audio content (videos with narration or dialogue)
Expected Result: Videos must include sign language interpretation visible on screen or activatable

Action: Verify visibility and size of sign language interpreter
Expected Result: Interpreter must be clearly visible, with adequate contrast and sufficient size to distinguish signs

Action: Verify synchronization between audio and sign language
Expected Result: Sign interpretation must be synchronized with spoken audio without significant delays

Test Summary: TC - [A11y] - 1.2 - Time-based Media - 1.2.7 - Extended Audio Description (Prerecorded)
Action: Identify videos where natural pauses are insufficient for audio description
Expected Result: Video must automatically pause to allow extended audio description of complex visual information

Action: Play video with extended audio description and verify pauses
Expected Result: Video must pause when needed to describe visual elements, then continue automatically

Action: Verify user control over extended audio description
Expected Result: User must be able to enable or disable extended audio description per preference

Action: Confirm that extended description does not interfere with content comprehension
Expected Result: Pauses and descriptions must integrate naturally without causing confusion or loss of narrative context

Action: Locate prerecorded videos and verify availability of complete text document
Expected Result: A complete text transcript must exist including dialogues, visual descriptions, and relevant sounds

Action: Review that text alternative describes complete sequence of visual events
Expected Result: Document must function as independent alternative, describing all essential visual information chronologically

Action: Verify accessibility of text alternative
Expected Result: Transcript must be in accessible format (HTML, structured document) near the original video

Action: Access live audio-only broadcast (podcast, web radio, conference)
Expected Result: An equivalent alternative must be presented synchronized with the live audio

Action: Verify delivery method of synchronized alternative
Expected Result: Alternative can be real-time transcript, synchronized text description, or updated equivalent content

Action: Verify that alternative captures essential information from live audio
Expected Result: Text content must reflect critical audio information with minimal acceptable delay

Action: Inspect HTML code of tables, lists, and headings with DevTools
Expected Result: Tables use thead/tbody/th with scope, lists use ul/ol/li, headings use h1-h6 hierarchically

Action: Verify forms with programmatically associated labels
Expected Result: Each input must have label with for attribute or be wrapped in label, visible to screen readers

Action: Review related field groups with fieldset and legend
Expected Result: Control groups (radio buttons, checkboxes) must be grouped with fieldset and descriptive legend

Action: Navigate the site using only Tab and verify reading order
Expected Result: Tab and DOM order must match the logical visual order of content

Action: Use screen reader to verify content reading order
Expected Result: Reading order must be logical and coherent, without confusing jumps caused by CSS positioning

Action: Identify instructions that use only shape, color, or position
Expected Result: Instructions must include descriptive text in addition to visual references (e.g., 'green Submit button' not just 'green button')

Action: Verify that critical information does not rely solely on sensory elements
Expected Result: Errors, alerts, or process steps must be communicated with text in addition to color, icons, or sounds

Action: Rotate device or change browser orientation
Expected Result: Content must work in portrait and landscape orientation without loss of functionality

Action: Verify that content is not locked to a single orientation
Expected Result: Application must not force specific orientation unless essential (e.g., piano app, bank check)

Action: Inspect form fields in source code
Expected Result: Common fields (name, email, phone) must have appropriate autocomplete attribute

Action: Test autocomplete with browser saved data
Expected Result: Fields with correct autocomplete must suggest user saved values when typing begins

Action: Review complex UI components and verify programmatic purpose
Expected Result: Components must identify their purpose through ARIA attributes or HTML semantics for assistive technologies

Action: Verify icons and interactive elements with accessibility inspector
Expected Result: Buttons, links, and icons must expose their function through appropriate roles and accessible names

Action: Identify elements where color conveys information (errors, states, links)
Expected Result: Information conveyed by color must include additional indicator (text, icon, pattern)

Action: Test site in grayscale mode or with color blindness simulator
Expected Result: All information must remain comprehensible without relying on color to distinguish elements

Action: Access pages with auto-playing audio
Expected Result: A visible control must exist to pause, stop, or mute audio playing for more than 3 seconds

Action: Verify that audio control is keyboard accessible
Expected Result: Control must be operable without mouse, using Tab and Enter/Space

Action: Measure text contrast with tool (e.g., Colour Contrast Analyser)
Expected Result: Normal text: minimum 4.5:1, large text (18pt+ or 14pt bold+): minimum 3:1

Action: Verify contrast in all states (normal, hover, focus, disabled)
Expected Result: All interactive element states must meet required contrast ratios

Action: Review text over images or gradient backgrounds
Expected Result: Overlaid text must have sufficient contrast throughout or use shadows/overlays

Action: Zoom to 200% in browser
Expected Result: Text must be readable and content accessible without horizontal scroll or overlaps

Action: Verify that controls and buttons remain operable when zoomed
Expected Result: Interactive elements must not be clipped or hidden when text size is increased

Action: Identify text rendered as image
Expected Result: Text must be in real HTML, not images (except logos or special cases)

Action: Inspect graphics and banners for embedded text
Expected Result: Titles, labels, and informative text must be CSS-styled HTML text, not images

Action: Measure text contrast in critical elements with analysis tools
Expected Result: Normal text: minimum 7:1, large text: minimum 4.5:1 for AAA compliance

Action: Document exceptions and plan contrast improvements
Expected Result: Identify areas not meeting AAA and prioritize changes in most critical elements

Test Summary: TC - [A11y] - 1.4 - Distinguishable - 1.4.7 - Low or No Background Audio
Action: Play audio content and evaluate background sound presence
Expected Result: Background audio must be removable, 20dB lower than main audio, or non-existent

Action: Verify control to disable background music if present
Expected Result: User must be able to mute or reduce ambient audio without affecting main narration

Action: Verify if user can customize spacing, column width, alignment, and fonts
Expected Result: System must allow visual presentation adjustments without loss of functionality or content

Action: Test readability with OS settings (text size, high contrast)
Expected Result: Content must respect user preferences defined at system level

Test Summary: TC - [A11y] - 1.4 - Distinguishable - 1.4.9 - Images of Text (No Exception)
Action: Thoroughly review entire site for text rendered as images
Expected Result: No text in image format must exist, all must be real HTML text (no exceptions except logos)

Action: Inspect decorative elements and verify text is styled with CSS
Expected Result: Visual text effects must be achieved with CSS, not by converting text to images

Action: Resize window to 320px width or zoom to 400%
Expected Result: Horizontal scrolling must not be required to read content (except complex tables)

Action: Test on real mobile device with narrow viewport
Expected Result: All content must adapt fluidly without clipping or need for bidirectional scrolling

Action: Measure contrast of UI components and focus states
Expected Result: Minimum 3:1 contrast for component borders, functional icons, and focus indicators

Action: Verify contrast of informative graphic elements (charts, status icons)
Expected Result: Visual elements conveying information must be clearly distinguishable from background

Action: Apply increased spacing CSS (line-height: 1.5, letter-spacing: 0.12em, etc.)
Expected Result: Content must remain readable and functional without clipping or overlaps

Action: Verify that buttons and controls do not break with modified spacing
Expected Result: Interactive elements must maintain functionality and readability with custom spacing

Test Summary: TC - [A11y] - 1.4 - Distinguishable - 1.4.13 - Content on Hover or Focus
Action: Activate tooltips and popup content with hover and keyboard
Expected Result: Additional content must be keyboard accessible, remain visible, and be dismissible without moving pointer

Action: Verify that popup content does not disappear when moving pointer over it
Expected Result: User must be able to move cursor over tooltip to read or interact without it disappearing

Action: Verify accessible dismiss mechanism (ESC or close button)
Expected Result: Popup content must be dismissible without moving pointer from the trigger that activated it

Action: Navigate entire site using only keyboard (Tab, Enter, Space, arrows)
Expected Result: All functions must be operable: menus, modals, forms, interactive controls

Action: Test complex components (accordions, tabs, calendars)
Expected Result: Custom widgets must respond to appropriate keys (arrows, Home, End, Escape)

Action: Verify all mouse actions have keyboard equivalent
Expected Result: Drag and drop, hover menus, touch gestures must have keyboard-operable alternatives

Test Summary: TC - [A11y] - 2.1 - Keyboard Accessible - 2.1.2 - No Keyboard Trap
Action: Navigate with Tab through all components
Expected Result: User must be able to exit any component using only keyboard (Tab, Shift+Tab, Esc)

Action: Test modals, dialogs, and overlays with keyboard navigation
Expected Result: Focus must move freely within component and exit without being trapped

Action: Verify that absolutely all functions are keyboard operable
Expected Result: No function should require mouse or gestures, all must have keyboard equivalent

Action: Document functions requiring specific pointer path
Expected Result: If function requires specific path (signature, drawing), keyboard alternative or alternative method must exist

Test Summary: TC - [A11y] - 2.1 - Keyboard Accessible - 2.1.4 - Character Key Shortcuts
Action: Test single character keyboard shortcuts if they exist
Expected Result: Shortcuts must be disableable, remappable, or only work when component has focus

Action: Verify shortcuts do not interfere with screen readers or assistive tools
Expected Result: Key combinations must avoid conflicts with assistive technology commands

Action: Identify timers or sessions with time limits
Expected Result: User must be able to extend, adjust, or disable time limits before expiration

Action: Verify prior warning before time expires
Expected Result: System must alert with sufficient advance notice and allow simple extension (e.g., press button)

Test Summary: TC - [A11y] - 2.2 - Enough Time - 2.2.2 - Pause, Stop, Hide
Action: Identify auto-moving content (carousels, animations)
Expected Result: Accessible control must exist to pause, stop, or hide content moving for more than 5 seconds

Action: Verify pause control is visible and keyboard operable
Expected Result: Pause/play buttons must be clearly visible and accessible without mouse

Action: Review all processes and verify existence of timeouts
Expected Result: No time limits should exist in any interaction, or timeless alternatives must exist

Action: Document exceptions where time is a real-time requirement
Expected Result: Identify live events or auctions where time limit is essential and cannot be removed

Action: Test system interruptions (notifications, automatic alerts)
Expected Result: User must be able to postpone or suppress interruptions except emergencies

Action: Verify notification preferences settings
Expected Result: System must offer controls to manage frequency and type of interruptions

Action: Let session expire and re-authenticate
Expected Result: Entered data must be preserved after re-authentication without information loss

Action: Test with long forms and verify data recovery
Expected Result: In-progress information must be auto-saved or recoverable after relogin

Action: Identify session timeouts and verify handling
Expected Result: System must warn about timeouts with sufficient notice and allow data saving

Action: Verify user can save work before timeout
Expected Result: Warning must include clear option to save progress or extend session

Test Summary: TC - [A11y] - 2.3 - Seizures and Physical Reactions - 2.3.1 - Three Flashes or Below Threshold
Action: Review site animations and transitions
Expected Result: No content should flash more than 3 times per second

Action: Use PEAT (Photosensitive Epilepsy Analysis Tool) if available
Expected Result: Analyze multimedia content to identify flashes that could cause seizures

Test Summary: TC - [A11y] - 2.3 - Seizures and Physical Reactions - 2.3.2 - Three Flashes
Action: Thoroughly analyze animations, videos, and dynamic content
Expected Result: No flash exceeding safe limits must exist, without exceptions

Action: Review embedded third-party content (ads, external videos)
Expected Result: External content must not contain dangerous flashes or must be disableable

Test Summary: TC - [A11y] - 2.3 - Seizures and Physical Reactions - 2.3.3 - Animation from Interactions
Action: Verify motion animations triggered by scroll or interaction
Expected Result: User must be able to disable non-essential motion animations

Action: Verify respect for system prefers-reduced-motion preference
Expected Result: Site must detect and respect OS setting to reduce motion

Action: Navigate with keyboard from page start
Expected Result: A 'Skip to main content' link must be visible on Tab press or appropriate ARIA landmarks

Action: Verify ARIA landmarks (main, nav, complementary, contentinfo)
Expected Result: Structure must use landmark roles to enable quick navigation with screen readers

Action: Review <title> element on each site page
Expected Result: Each page must have a unique descriptive title identifying its content

Action: Verify title follows consistent format (content - section - site name)
Expected Result: Titles must follow predictable pattern facilitating identification in tabs and history

Action: Navigate with Tab in order and verify visual logic
Expected Result: Focus order must follow logical sequence left to right, top to bottom

Action: Identify elements with positive tabindex and evaluate necessity
Expected Result: Avoid tabindex greater than 0, order must be controlled through logical DOM structure

Action: Review all site links
Expected Result: Link text or surrounding context must clearly describe its destination (avoid 'click here')

Action: Use screen reader to list all links
Expected Result: Links must be understandable when presented out of context in link list

Action: Try to locate an internal page in different ways
Expected Result: Navigation menu, internal search, sitemap, or breadcrumbs must exist

Action: Verify at least two navigation methods are available
Expected Result: User must be able to choose between different strategies to locate content

Action: Inspect headings (h1-h6) and form labels
Expected Result: Headings must clearly describe sections, labels must identify field purpose

Action: Use screen reader to navigate by headings
Expected Result: Heading structure must allow quick understanding of content organization

Action: Navigate with Tab and observe focus indicator
Expected Result: Interactive elements must show clear contrasted visual indicator when focused

Action: Verify outline is not disabled without visual replacement
Expected Result: If default outline is removed, equally visible alternative indicator must exist

Action: Navigate through different site sections and verify location indicators
Expected Result: Breadcrumbs, section titles, or indicators showing user location must exist

Action: Review that breadcrumbs are accessible and reflect correct hierarchy
Expected Result: Breadcrumbs must be keyboard navigable and structured with ordered list

Action: Review link text in isolation, without surrounding context
Expected Result: Each link must be understandable by itself without need for additional context

Action: Identify generic links ('more info', 'read more') and improve them
Expected Result: Ambiguous links must include additional context via aria-label or aria-describedby

Action: Review content structure and heading usage
Expected Result: Content must be organized with descriptive headings dividing information into logical sections

Action: Verify no long text blocks exist without intermediate headings
Expected Result: Extensive content must be subdivided with headings facilitating scanning and navigation

Action: Navigate with keyboard and verify focus visibility on elements near edges
Expected Result: At least part of focus indicator must remain visible, not completely hidden by other elements

Action: Test navigation in areas with sticky headers or floating elements
Expected Result: Sticky bars must not completely cover the focused element

Action: Verify focus is completely visible in all situations
Expected Result: Focus indicator must be completely visible without any obstruction by other elements

Action: Implement auto-scroll when element receives focus outside viewport
Expected Result: Content must auto-adjust to fully show the focused element

Action: Measure focus indicator area and contrast with specialized tools
Expected Result: Indicator must have sufficient minimum area and high contrast for maximum visibility

Action: Calculate that indicator has minimum 2 CSS pixels perimeter or equivalent area
Expected Result: Indicator thickness and area must meet specific WCAG 2.2 requirements

Action: Identify functions activated by complex gestures (swipe, pinch)
Expected Result: Functions must have alternative with simple single-point gestures (tap, click)

Action: Verify carousels, maps, and swipeable components
Expected Result: Alternative buttons or controls must exist for users with motor limitations

Action: Test activation of interactive elements
Expected Result: Function must not execute on mousedown/touchstart, must allow cancellation by releasing outside target

Action: Verify accidental clicks can be cancelled by dragging outside
Expected Result: User must be able to undo accidental activation by moving pointer before release

Test Summary: TC - [A11y] - 2.5 - Input Modalities - 2.5.3 - Label in Name
Action: Inspect controls with visible labels using screen reader
Expected Result: Accessible name of control must include text of its visible label

Action: Verify voice commands can activate controls using visible text
Expected Result: Voice control users must be able to say visible text to activate element

Action: Identify functions using accelerometer or gyroscope
Expected Result: Functions activated by device motion must have standard UI alternative

Action: Verify setting to disable motion actuation
Expected Result: User must be able to disable motion responses to avoid accidental activations

Test Summary: TC - [A11y] - 2.5 - Input Modalities - 2.5.6 - Concurrent Input Mechanisms
Action: Test site with multiple simultaneous input devices (keyboard + mouse + touch)
Expected Result: Site must support simultaneous use of different input mechanisms without restrictions

Action: Verify switching input method does not cause loss of functionality
Expected Result: User must be able to freely switch between keyboard, mouse, and touch without issues

Action: Identify drag and drop functions
Expected Result: Drag and drop functions must have alternative with simple clicks or taps

Action: Test keyboard alternatives for reordering elements
Expected Result: Reorderable lists must be manipulable with keyboard (arrows + Space/Enter)

Action: Measure dimensions of touch controls (buttons, links)
Expected Result: Touch targets must have minimum 24x24 CSS pixels or equivalent spacing

Action: Verify spacing between adjacent touch targets
Expected Result: If targets are smaller than 24px, sufficient surrounding space must exist to avoid errors

Action: Inspect lang attribute on <html> tag
Expected Result: Lang attribute must be present and specify correct language of main content

Action: Verify correct pronunciation with screen reader
Expected Result: Reader must use voice and pronunciation rules appropriate to declared language

Action: Identify text fragments in language different from main
Expected Result: Elements with language change must have appropriate lang attribute

Action: Test screen reader reading of quotes or terms in another language
Expected Result: Language changes must be recognized and pronounced correctly

Action: Identify jargon, technical terms, or unusual words in content
Expected Result: Glossary, inline definitions, or mechanism to explain uncommon terms must exist

Action: Verify accessibility of definitions through links or dfn/abbr elements
Expected Result: Specialized terms must link to accessible explanations

Action: Locate abbreviations and acronyms in content
Expected Result: First instance of abbreviations must include full expansion or link to definition

Action: Implement abbr element with title attribute where appropriate
Expected Result: Abbreviations must be semantically marked for assistive technologies

Action: Evaluate text complexity and required reading level
Expected Result: Complex content must have simplified version or summary for lower secondary education level

Action: Use readability analysis tools (Flesch-Kincaid, INFLESZ)
Expected Result: Measure readability indices and provide alternatives for technical or complex content

Action: Identify words where pronunciation is critical for comprehension
Expected Result: Mechanism to indicate pronunciation must exist (IPA, audio, phonetic text)

Action: Verify homographs and ambiguous terms with pronunciation guides
Expected Result: Words with multiple pronunciations must be clarified when context is insufficient

Action: Navigate with Tab through interactive elements
Expected Result: No element should cause context change (navigation, submission) just by receiving focus

Action: Verify form fields do not submit when focused
Expected Result: Focus must be independent of activation, changes must only occur with explicit action

Action: Interact with forms and controls without activating submit button
Expected Result: Field changes must not cause context change without prior warning or confirmation

Action: Test selects and radios that may cause automatic navigation
Expected Result: If change causes action, prior warning or confirmation button must be required

Action: Compare menus and navigation across different pages
Expected Result: Repeated navigation elements must appear in same order across all pages

Action: Document location of global elements (search, login, main menu)
Expected Result: Common components must maintain consistent position and order throughout site

Action: Identify components with same function across different pages
Expected Result: Equivalent components must use same labels, icons, and accessible names

Action: Verify consistency of icons and symbols throughout site
Expected Result: Same icon must represent same function, avoid different symbols for identical actions

Action: Verify significant changes require explicit user action
Expected Result: Context changes, submissions, or navigation must not occur without explicit confirmation

Action: Identify and fix automatic submissions or unexpected redirects
Expected Result: User must initiate all important changes through clear buttons or links

Action: Search for help mechanisms in different sections
Expected Result: Help options must appear in consistent location throughout site

Action: Verify quick access to contact, FAQ, chat, or documentation
Expected Result: Help must be available predictably in fixed location

Action: Submit form with intentional errors
Expected Result: Errors must be clearly identified in text, visually marked, and announced to screen readers

Action: Verify error messages use aria-describedby or aria-invalid
Expected Result: Errors must be programmatically associated with affected fields for assistive technologies

Action: Verify focus moves to first error or error summary
Expected Result: After validation, user must be clearly directed to found problems

Test Summary: TC - [A11y] - 3.3 - Input Assistance - 3.3.2 - Labels or Instructions
Action: Review all form fields
Expected Result: Each field must have visible and programmatically associated label, with clear instructions if needed

Action: Verify fields with specific formats (date, phone, zip code)
Expected Result: Fields with format requirements must include examples or input masks

Action: Trigger validation errors in forms
Expected Result: Error messages must suggest how to fix the specific problem

Action: Evaluate message quality: must be specific and solution-oriented
Expected Result: Errors must explain what is wrong and how to fix it, not just indicate there is an error

Test Summary: TC - [A11y] - 3.3 - Input Assistance - 3.3.4 - Error Prevention (Legal, Financial, Data)
Action: Complete financial or legal transactions if they exist
Expected Result: Must allow reviewing, confirming, and correcting information before final submission

Action: Verify confirmation page with option to edit before final submission
Expected Result: Critical data must be shown in summary with ability to go back and modify

Action: Search for contextual help in forms and complex tasks
Expected Result: Available help must exist to complete tasks, visible or easily accessible

Action: Verify tooltips, help links, or info icons next to complex fields
Expected Result: Help must be available in context where needed, not only on separate page

Action: Identify forms that modify, delete, or compromise user data
Expected Result: Reversible mechanism (undo), verification (review before submit), or explicit confirmation must exist

Action: Test form submission with critical information
Expected Result: System must present review screen where user can verify and correct before confirming

Action: Verify availability of option to revert completed actions
Expected Result: Destructive actions must allow undo or have grace period before permanent execution

Action: Verify explicit confirmations for irreversible actions
Expected Result: Deletions, transfers, or permanent changes must require clear and specific additional confirmation

Action: Complete multi-step forms
Expected Result: Previously provided information must auto-complete or be selectable, not re-entered

Action: Verify data from previous steps is pre-filled in later steps
Expected Result: User must not re-type name, address, or other already entered data

Action: Test login and authentication process
Expected Result: Must offer accessible alternatives to visual CAPTCHA or cognitive tests (email, SMS, biometrics)

Action: Verify availability of magic link or OTP code authentication
Expected Result: Alternative methods must be equally secure without requiring visual recognition

Action: Evaluate all available authentication methods
Expected Result: System must offer methods not relying on object recognition or complex cognitive tests

Action: Document and promote use of biometric authentication or password managers
Expected Result: Authentication options must include AAA methods not requiring complex memory

Action: Validate HTML with W3C Validator or similar tool
Expected Result: No critical errors must exist: duplicate IDs, improperly nested tags, missing required attributes

Action: Review dynamically generated code with browser inspector
Expected Result: JavaScript generating content must produce valid and well-formed HTML

Action: Inspect custom controls with accessibility inspector
Expected Result: All controls must expose correct name, role, and value to accessibility API

Action: Test custom widgets with screen reader
Expected Result: Components must announce correctly with appropriate ARIA role, state, and properties

Action: Verify state changes are reflected in ARIA attributes
Expected Result: Dynamic states (expanded, selected, pressed) must be updated programmatically

Action: Trigger dynamic messages (confirmation, error, loading)
Expected Result: Messages must be announced in screen readers using appropriate ARIA live regions

Action: Verify correct usage of role=alert, aria-live=polite/assertive
Expected Result: Critical messages use assertive, informational use polite, without moving focus

Action: Test with screen reader that announcements interrupt or wait appropriately
Expected Result: Live regions must be configured by message urgency without causing excessive interruptions
